mnemonics: OPEN is O-P-E-N: Open sets are Points with Entire Neighborhoods inside them. For any point, you can find a small enough 'circle' (ball) that stays completely within the set.
keyPoints: An open set is a set where every point is an interior point.
The union of any collection of open sets is itself open.
The intersection of a finite number of open sets is open.
commonMistakes: Assuming the intersection of infinitely many open sets is always open (it is not).
Confusing an open set with its complement, a closed set.
